summary.intent: Address Codex automated review findings on PR #680 in fogodev/ars-ui, fix two slider state-sync edge cases, and get the PR approved.
summary.outcome: Fixed two real bugs in the slider component (non-finite controlled value dropping controlled mode, and active drag state not resnapping when props change mid-drag), all tests passed (6456 unit + integration), spec validated, fast CI gate cleared, and changes committed as 'Reconcile slider controlled drag prop sync'.

repo: The project uses a `cargo xci-fast` fast gate with at least 10 sub-gates (fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, etc.) that must all pass before committing.
Spec lives alongside code in `spec/components/input/slider.md` and must be kept in sync with implementation; `cargo xtask spec validate` enforces this.
GitHub PR review threads are addressed by fetching live GraphQL thread IDs — cached/snapshot database IDs become stale after review updates and cause reply failures.
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` comment and polled via a loop watching for 👀 (in-progress) → 👍 (approved) reactions and unresolved thread count.
Commits co-authored by Codex use the trailer `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>`.

code.finding: SyncValue state transition did not preserve controlled mode when the incoming controlled prop was non-finite; patched to stay in controlled mode using the current effective value.
SetProps handler did not resnap active drag state against updated output props (step, bounds), causing stale committed values mid-drag; patched to reconcile drag value on prop change.
Spec sections for SyncValue and SetProps were updated to document the corrected controlled-mode and drag-reconciliation behaviors.

workflow: When replying to GitHub review threads via API, always refresh live GraphQL thread IDs rather than using cached snapshot IDs, as GitHub reassigns comment database IDs after review updates.
Write failing regression tests before patching implementation to confirm the defect is real and the fix is targeted.
`cargo +nightly fmt --all` must be run before the fast gate if new code blocks were added, as fmt is the first gate and will fail otherwise.
Polling a Codex review loop every 120 seconds with exit conditions on 👍 or new unresolved threads is the standard wait pattern for this repo.

friction: Stale database IDs from the earlier review snapshot caused all six initial inline replies to fail; required a live GraphQL re-fetch to unblock.
Cargo test runner does not accept multiple test filter arguments in one invocation; had to use the broader `input::slider` module filter.
The `SetProps` patch introduced a Rust formatting violation that caused the fast gate to fail on the first rerun, requiring a separate `cargo +nightly fmt` pass before continuing.
open_items: The PR is awaiting another Codex review pass after the latest commit; approval is not yet confirmed.
The two patched edge cases (non-finite controlled value and mid-drag prop change) may have analogous issues in other bindable input components (e.g., number input, range) that were not audited.
